commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a per-menu closed-content signature cache to skip repeated invisible `populateMenu` work when observation churn does not change rendered menu content, plus three focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: broad menu observation can invalidate closed menus, and current main repopulates attached closed menus whenever their versions differ. I did not run live profiling in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for inspectable real behavior proof; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue from this review.

target.title: Skip redundant closed-menu rebuilds when displayed content is unchanged
target.body:
## Problem

CodexBar pre-warms closed (invisible) persistent menus so they open instantly. `observeStoreChanges` fires `invalidateMenus()` on every change of `store.menuObservationToken`, and for closed menus that runs `prepareAttachedClosedMenusIfNeeded()` → a full main-thread `populateMenu()` for **every** attached menu (status item, merged, fallback, each provider menu).

`menuObservationToken` is a signature over ~25 store properties (probe logs, in-flight refresh flags, revision counters, dashboard timestamps…). Many of these change frequently — e.g. while you're actively using codex/claude and CodexBar is watching their data — but **don't change what the menu displays**. So the whole SwiftUI menu hierarchy rebuilds on the main thread continuously with no visible change.

Observed on a real 0.32.4 install during active use: sustained ~12% "idle" CPU, with `sample` showing `rebuildClosedMenuIfNeeded → populateMenu` (→ `addOverviewRows` / `costHistoryInlineDashboard`) as the dominant main-thread stack (~18% of main-thread samples), worsening over time as usage history grows. It's independent of refresh-interval and token-cost settings (confirmed by changing both with no effect), since it's driven by store observation, not the refresh timer.

## Fix

Gate the closed-menu rebuild on a content signature (the existing `menuAdjunctReadinessSignature` plus the per-provider usage snapshot). When the displayed content is unchanged since the menu was last pre-warmed, skip the expensive `populateMenu` and leave the menu marked stale so `menuWillOpen` still rebuilds it fresh.

Safe by construction: the open path (`refreshMenuForOpenIfNeeded`) is untouched and always rebuilds on `menuWillOpen`, so an incomplete signature can only skip a pre-warm — never show stale content to the user.

## Evidence

- **Controlled (new test):** a stream of observation changes with unchanged displayed content produced **39 redundant full rebuilds** before the change; after, it **converges to ≤2 and stops** (20+ further ticks add zero rebuilds). A genuine content change still rebuilds.
- **End-to-end (new test):** driving the real `invalidateMenus` entry point against an attached closed menu no longer storms.
- Full test suite green (3241 tests).

## Tests added

- `closed menu rebuild converges when displayed content is unchanged`
- `closed menu rebuilds again when displayed content changes`
- `invalidateMenus does not storm a closed attached menu on unchanged content`
